title: Pyrus elongata
taxonomy: Pyrus elongata is a species of pear tree that belongs to the family Rosaceae. This classification places it within the order Rosales, which also includes other flowering plants such as roses and apples. The genus Pyrus comprises several species of pear trees, and Pyrus elongata is one of them. Its classification helps botanists understand its relationships with other plants and its evolutionary history. By grouping it with other related species, researchers can better comprehend the diversity of plants within the Rosaceae family.

preparations: Pyrus elongata, also known as the Himalayan pear, is a plant species used in traditional medicine in the Himalayan region. intro: Pyrus elongata, also known as the Korean pear or Korean mountain pear, is a deciduous tree species native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are thought to aid in digestion, boost the immune system, and even exhibit anti-cancer activities. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus elongata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pyrus elongata is commonly prepared into teas, infusions, and juices, and is also used to make dried extracts and powder for use in supplements and topical applications.
